column1: # OWASP ZAP baseline scan rule overrides.
# The baseline action is WARN; we tighten clinically-relevant rules to FAIL and
# silence ones that produce noise without value on a private clinical API.
# Always FAIL the build on anything that exposes patient data without auth — these
# rules surface when authentication / authorization is misconfigured.
# Mixed-content / HTTPS rules — FAIL in production scans, allow in dev where TLS is off.
# Anti-CSRF — informational on a SPA-only API where every mutating endpoint requires
# a Bearer JWT (CSRF is irrelevant when there's no cookie-based session). Silence
# the noisy rule on the API surface, but keep it FAIL on any HTML page returned by
# Cookie attributes (Secure / HttpOnly / SameSite). Always FAIL — even the BFF auth
# Information disclosure — server header, X-Powered-By, etc. SecurityHeaders middleware
# already strips these; FAIL if a regression sneaks them back in.
